source: National Health Mission, Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, Government of India

protocols.name: Obstetric Hemorrhage (Excessive Bleeding)
danger_signs: Heavy vaginal bleeding (soaking more than 1 pad per hour)
Placenta not delivered within 30 minutes of birth
immediate_actions: Call 108 IMMEDIATELY
Keep patient lying flat — raise legs if possible
Apply uterine massage if postpartum
Do NOT give anything by mouth
Keep patient warm — prevent shock
Maintain IV line if trained
Do not waste time — transport urgently to FRU

protocols.name: Eclampsia / Seizures in Pregnancy or Postpartum
danger_signs: Convulsions/fits in a pregnant or newly delivered woman
Severe headache not relieved by medication
immediate_actions: Call 108 IMMEDIATELY
Do NOT put anything in patient's mouth
Move patient away from sharp objects
Turn patient to left side to prevent aspiration
After seizure stops — keep on left side
If MgSO4 available and trained — administer per protocol
Do NOT give oral medications during/after seizure

protocols.name: Severe Respiratory Distress
danger_signs: Chest indrawing (skin pulls in between ribs when breathing)
Inability to complete sentences due to breathlessness
immediate_actions: Call 108 IMMEDIATELY
Sit patient upright to ease breathing
Loosen tight clothing
Keep patient calm
Do NOT give food or water
Transport urgently to hospital

protocols.name: Loss of Consciousness / Altered Sensorium
danger_signs: Unconscious — not responding to voice or touch
Weakness of arm or leg on one side
immediate_actions: Call 108 IMMEDIATELY
Do NOT give anything by mouth
Recovery position (left side)
Clear airway — tilt head back, lift chin
Check breathing and pulse
Begin CPR if no pulse and not breathing (if trained)
Transport urgently

protocols.name: Puerperal Sepsis (Infection After Delivery)
danger_signs: High fever (> 38°C) within 10 days of delivery
Rapid pulse with low blood pressure
immediate_actions: Call 108 IMMEDIATELY
Do NOT delay transport
Keep patient hydrated with oral fluids if conscious
Transport urgently to FRU/District Hospital
Sepsis is life-threatening — do not wait for improvement

general_asha_guidelines: ALWAYS carry your ASHA kit bag including thermometer, BP apparatus, ORS packets
Keep 108 number saved in phone — share with all pregnant women in your area
Never delay transport for any obstetric emergency — load and go
Register all pregnancies within first trimester in your area
Ensure 4 ANC visits for all pregnant women
Identify and refer high-risk pregnancies to PHC/CHC
